what_it_does: An empty Git placeholder file that causes the parent directory to be tracked by version control even when it contains no other files.
observations: The file content is completely empty.
The filename is .gitkeep, a common Git convention.
It sits in the company/mcp_face/ directory.
conventions: Follows the .gitkeep convention for preserving empty directories in Git.
Contains no code, configuration, or documentation.
notes: This is just a directory-preservation stub for mcp_face; there is no executable or design content here.
questions: What will the mcp_face directory eventually contain?
Is this directory intentionally empty, or awaiting future assets?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (keep directory in version control) matches the empty .gitkeep content.
intention_signals: It appears intended to reserve the mcp_face directory in the repository so it is not lost by Git's empty-directory pruning.
contribution: It contributes a tracked directory placeholder, ensuring the mcp_face path survives in a fused or cloned system even when no other files are present.
summary_for_embedding: An empty .gitkeep file in company/mcp_face/ that preserves the otherwise empty directory under Git version control.
intention_for_embedding: To keep the mcp_face directory tracked by Git until substantive files are added.
